I need to understand ALL the ways to elevate permissions or bypass permission prompts in Claude Code. The user already knows about:
- allowedTools in settings.json
- settings.json hierarchy (global/local/project)
- Plan Mode auto-approval

What other mechanisms exist? Specifically looking for:
- The `--dangerously-skip-permissions` flag or similar CLI flags
- Environment variables like `CLAUDE_CODE_INSECURE_SKIP_PERMISSIONS`
- `alwaysAllow` or `autoAllow` settings
- Permission modes (default/accept-edits/bypass)
- Any hidden or advanced permission configurations
- Hooks that can auto-approve
- The `permissionMode` setting in settings.json

Report back everything you find about permission elevation in Claude Code. Be thorough.

Web search results for query: "Claude Code permission elevation bypass settings dangerously-skip-permissions permissionMode 2025 2026"

Links: [{"title":"CVE-2026-33068 / anthropic / 8.8 / patch","url":"https://feedly.com/cve/CVE-2026-33068"},{"title":"Permissions - Claude Code","url":"https://www.mintlify.com/saurav-shakya/Claude_Code-_Source_Code/concepts/permissions"},{"title":"Claude Code auto mode: a safer way to skip permissions","url":"https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/claude-code-auto-mode"},{"title":"SecuriTricks - CVE-2026-33068","url":"https://securitricks.com/cve/CVE-2026-33068"},{"title":"Claude Code Auto Mode: Autonomy Without the Risk","url":"https://institute.sfeir.com/en/articles/claude-code-auto-mode-permissions-autonomy/"},{"title":"Bypass permissions check uses wrong permission_mode string · Issue #67 · banyudu/claude-warden","url":"https://github.com/banyudu/claude-warden/issues/67"},{"title":"Claude Code adds an automated permission determination feature, where AI checks permission risks and automatically executes tasks.","url":"https://gigazine.net/gsc_news/en/20260325-claude-auto-mode"},{"title":"CVE-2026-33068 - Vulnerability-Lookup","url":"https://cve.circl.lu/vuln/CVE-2026-33068"},{"title":"Nixpkgs security tracker","url":"https://tracker.security.nixos.org/issues/NIXPKGS-2026-0681"},{"title":"CVE-2026-33068: CWE-807: Reliance on Untrusted Inputs in a Security Decision in anthropics claude-code - Live Threat Intelligence - Threat Radar | OffSeq.com","url":"https://radar.offseq.com/threat/cve-2026-33068-cwe-807-reliance-on-untrusted-input-81883495"}]

Here's a summary of what the search reveals about this topic:

---

## CVE-2026-33068 — Claude Code Workspace Trust Dialog Bypass

This is a **HIGH-severity vulnerability** (CVSS 7.7–8.8) discovered in Claude Code versions **prior to 2.1.53**, published in March 2026.

### The Vulnerability

Claude Code resolved permission mode settings from repository-controlled `.claude/settings.json` files **before** displaying the workspace trust confirmation dialog. A malicious repository could set:

```json
{
  "permissions": {
    "defaultMode": "bypassPermissions"
  }
}
```

This caused the trust dialog to be **silently skipped** when a user first opened the repository, placing them into full permissive mode without any prompt or explicit consent.

### Impact

- **No trust prompt appeared** — users were unaware they'd entered `bypassPermissions` mode
- Attacker-controlled repos could gain **unrestricted tool execution** (shell commands, file writes, web requests) without user awareness
- CVSS 4.0 score of **7.7** (Network vector, Low complexity, HIGH impact on CIA triad)
- Classified as **CWE-807**: Reliance on Untrusted Inputs in a Security Decision

### The Fix

- **Patched in Claude Code version 2.1.53** and later
- The fix ensures the trust dialog appears **before** repository settings are resolved
- Users on auto-update received the fix automatically; manual updaters should verify with `claude --version`

---

## Broader Context: Permission Modes

Claude Code has **five/six permission modes** (as of 2026):

| Mode | Behavior |
|------|----------|
| `default` | Prompts for destructive operations |
| `plan` | Read-only; shows plan first |
| `acceptEdits` | Auto-approves file edits, still prompts for commands |
| `auto` | New classifier-based safety (March 2026, Team plan) |
| `dontAsk` | Skips most prompts, retains some checks |
| `bypassPermissions` | **No checks at all** — every tool auto-approved |

The `--dangerously-skip-permissions` CLI flag sets the `bypassPermissions` mode. Notably, the **internal enum value** sent by Claude Code is `"bypassPermissions"`, not `"dangerously-skip-permissions"` — a distinction that caused a bug in the `claude-warden` tool (Issue #67).

### Auto Mode (March 2026)

Anthropic introduced **auto mode** as a safer middle ground between manual approvals and fully bypassing permissions. It uses a Sonnet 4.6 classifier to evaluate each action before execution with:
- A fast single-token filter (Stage 1)
- Chain-of-thought reasoning on flagged actions (Stage 2)
- ~0.4% false-positive rate on benign traffic
- ~17% false-negative rate on real overeager actions

---

## Mitigation Recommendations

1. **Update to Claude Code ≥ 2.1.53** immediately
2. Be cautious when opening repositories from untrusted sources
3. Check for `.claude/settings.json` in any cloned repo and review what it specifies
4. Never use `--dangerously-skip-permissions` outside isolated containers/VM


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

Links: [{"title":"权限覆盖与强制初始化","url":"https://www.cnblogs.com/alibaba00/p/19669693#commentform"},{"title":"claude_settings 0.4.3","url":"https://docs.rs/crate/claude_settings/0.4.3"},{"title":"配置自动模式 - Claude Code Docs","url":"https://code.claude.com/docs/zh-CN/auto-mode-config"},{"title":"如何给Claude Code授权操作当前项目的“所有权限”？ 原创","url":"https://blog.csdn.net/zhizaibide1987/article/details/158853323"},{"title":"Environment is context: security auditing for AI agent workstations","url":"https://dev.to/t3chn/environment-is-context-security-auditing-for-ai-agent-workstations-1li8#comments"},{"title":"Permissions and Security - Cheatsheet","url":"https://institute.sfeir.com/en/claude-code/claude-code-permissions-and-security/cheatsheet/"},{"title":"还是Claude Code干活靠谱啊 工程化配置与工作流实践","url":"https://segmentfault.com/a/1190000047713135"},{"title":"GitHub - dwillitzer/claude-settings: Claude Code settings and permissions configuration · GitHub","url":"https://github.com/dwillitzer/claude-settings"},{"title":"Struct Settings Copy item path","url":"https://docs.rs/claude_settings/0.4.3/claude_settings/types/struct.Settings.html"},{"title":"Auto mode for Claude Code","url":"https://simonwillison.net/2026/Mar/24/auto-mode-for-claude-code/?utm_source=weeklyfoo&utm_medium=web&utm_campaign=weeklyfoo-130&ref=weeklyfoo"}]

## 搜索结果摘要

根据搜索结果，以下是关于 Claude Code `settings.json` 权限配置和自动模式的详细信息：

---

### 配置层级（优先级从高到低）

| 层级 | 文件位置 | 说明 |
|------|----------|------|
| 组织强制 | `managed-settings.json` | 最高优先级，不可覆盖 |
| 命令行参数 | `--allow` 等标志 | 单次覆盖 |
| 项目个人 | `.claude/settings.local.json` | 个人偏好，应加入 `.gitignore` |
| 项目共享 | `.claude/settings.json` | 团队共享，提交到 Git |
| 全局个人 | `~/.claude/settings.json` | 最低优先级 |

---

### 权限系统：`allow` / `ask` / `deny`

在 `settings.json` 的 `permissions` 块中配置：

```json
{
  "permissions": {
    "allow": [
      "Bash(git status)",
      "Bash(git diff)",
      "Bash(npm test)",
      "Read",
      "Write"
    ],
    "deny": [
      "Bash(rm -rf *)",
      "Bash(curl * | bash)",
      "Bash(chmod 777)"
    ]
  }
}
```

- **allow**：无需确认即可自动运行
- **deny**：永远禁止（优先级高于 allow）
- **ask**（默认）：每次询问确认

---

### 自动模式（Auto Mode）

自动模式是 `--dangerously-skip-permissions` 的替代方案，通过 `autoMode` 设置块配置：

```json
{
  "autoMode": {
    "environment": [
      "$defaults",
      "Source control: github.example.com/acme-corp and all repos under it"
    ],
    "allow": ["$defaults", "..."],
    "soft_deny": ["$defaults", "..."]
  }
}
```

- 分类器使用 **Claude Sonnet 4.6** 来判断每个操作
- `claude auto-mode defaults` 打印内置规则
- `claude auto-mode config` 查看生效配置

---

### 关于 `alwaysAllow`、`autoAllow` 和 `CLAUDE_CODE_INSECURE` 的说明

⚠️ 本次搜索**未在主要文档中直接找到** `alwaysAllow`、`autoAllow` 和 `CLAUDE_CODE_INSECURE` 环境变量的官方文档。这些可能是：

- **较新的实验性功能**，尚未广泛收录
- **社区/非官方术语**：`alwaysAllow` 可能指代 `permissions.allow` 配置；`autoAllow` 可能与 `autoMode.allow` 相关
- **`CLAUDE_CODE_INSECURE`**：可能是一个环境变量，用于跳过某些安全校验（类似于 `--dangerously-skip-permissions` 的效果），但未在本次搜索结果中出现

建议直接在 **[code.claude.com/docs](https://code.claude.com/docs)** 官方文档中搜索这些具体术语，或检查 Claude Code 的更新日志（changelog）获取最新信息。

Links: [{"title":"feat: implement PreToolUse hook for tool auto-approval and restriction logic · Issue #707 · EdanStarfire/claudecode_webui","url":"https://github.com/EdanStarfire/claudecode_webui/issues/707"},{"title":"Permissions","url":"https://hexdocs.pm/claude_code/0.34.0/permissions.html"},{"title":"Permissions - Claude Code","url":"https://www.mintlify.com/saurav-shakya/Claude_Code-_Source_Code/concepts/permissions"},{"title":"Permissions and Security - Cheatsheet","url":"https://institute.sfeir.com/en/claude-code/claude-code-permissions-and-security/cheatsheet/"},{"title":"nyolo","url":"https://www.npmjs.com/package/nyolo"},{"title":"Permissions and Security","url":"https://institute.sfeir.com/en/claude-code/claude-code-permissions-and-security/"},{"title":"Claude Code auto mode: a safer way to skip permissions","url":"https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/claude-code-auto-mode"},{"title":"HandleClaudeHook auto-allow permission decision is completely untested · Issue #19 · gregology/sctx","url":"https://github.com/gregology/sctx/issues/19"},{"title":"Configure permissions - Claude Code Docs","url":"https://code.claude.com/docs/en/agent-sdk/permissions"},{"title":"Claude Code power user customization: How to configure hooks | Claude","url":"https://claude.com/blog/how-to-configure-hooks?1e959936_page=2&b7eea976_page=2&d7430fcd_page=2"}]

Here's a summary of what I found regarding **Claude Code hooks** and **PreToolUse auto-approve / skip_permissions**:

---

## PreToolUse Hook

The **PreToolUse** hook fires *after* Claude chooses a tool but *before* the tool actually executes. It's the most commonly used hook for implementing custom, dynamic tool access control. Your hook script can inspect the planned action and return:

- **`approve`** — tool executes without permission prompt
- **`deny`** — tool is blocked
- **`prompt`** — fall back to normal permission flow (user gets prompted)

---

## How Permissions Are Evaluated (Order of Precedence)

1. **Hooks** — Run first; can allow, deny, or defer
2. **Deny rules** — Check `disallowed_tools` and `settings.json` deny rules (block even in `bypassPermissions` mode)
3. **Permission mode** — e.g., `bypassPermissions`, `acceptEdits`, `default`
4. **Allow rules** — Check `allowed_tools` and `settings.json` allow rules
5. **`canUseTool` callback** — Final fallback for interactive approval

---

## Configuration Example

Hooks are configured in `.claude/settings.json` or `~/.claude/settings.json`:

```json
{
  "hooks": {
    "PreToolUse": [
      {
        "matcher": "Bash",
        "hooks": [
          {
            "type": "command",
            "command": "/path/to/my-approval-script.sh"
          }
        ]
      }
    ]
  }
}
```

The hook script receives a JSON payload on **stdin** with the tool name, arguments, and context, and returns a JSON decision via **stdout**. Exit codes determine behavior: **0 = allow**, **2 = block**, other = allow with warning.

---

## Use Cases for PreToolUse Auto-Approve

- **Conditional auto-approval**: Auto-approve `Bash` with `echo` but require approval for `rm`
- **Context-aware restrictions**: Deny `Write` if session is in "read-only" mode
- **Agent-type specific rules**: Builders get more access; testers get restrictions
- **Rate limiting / quota control**: Deny expensive tools when limits are hit
- **Security policies**: Block tools in production contexts

---

## Key Tools & Packages

- **`nyolo`** (npm): A PreToolUse hook that auto-blocks dangerous operations (`rm -rf`, `sudo`, `DROP TABLE`, `git push --force`, etc.) while running with `--dangerously-skip-permissions`. Install via `npx nyolo install`.
- **`--dangerously-skip-permissions`**: CLI flag that enables `bypassPermissions` mode — skips all permission prompts (use only in sandboxed/CI environments).
- **Auto mode**: Anthropic's newer model-classified approval system using Sonnet 4.6 as a classifier (safer middle-ground).

---

Would you like me to dig deeper into any specific aspect, such as the hook JSON schema, script examples, or `nyolo` configuration?

Links: [{"title":"Claude Code auto mode: a safer way to skip permissions","url":"https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/claude-code-auto-mode"},{"title":"Claude Code Auto Mode","url":"https://shellypalmer.com/2026/03/claude-code-auto-mode/"},{"title":"Permissions - Claude Code","url":"https://www.mintlify.com/saurav-shakya/Claude_Code-_Source_Code/concepts/permissions"},{"title":"Claude --dangerously-skip-permissions | AI Wiki","url":"https://aiwiki.ai/wiki/claude_--dangerously-skip-permissions"},{"title":"🚀 Claude Just Fixed the Biggest Annoyance with AI Coding Assistants (Meet Auto Mode)","url":"https://dev.to/siddhesh_surve/claude-just-fixed-the-biggest-annoyance-with-ai-coding-assistants-meet-auto-mode-2hm1#comments"},{"title":"Auto mode for Claude Code | Claude","url":"https://claude.com/ko-kr/blog/auto-mode?CampaignId=QS_WebConv&SiteId=DP_QS&CampaignName=GLOBAL-GLOBAL%5BEN%5D_QS-WebConv%5BGEN%5D-DP-EMFin-AdvancedProfile_MT-Image_AL-Generic&AdId=EMFin_Funding-Details&utm_medium=cpc&utm_source=qs&utm_campaign=global-global%5Ben%5D_qs-webconv%5Bgen%5D-dp-emfin-advancedprofile_mt-image_al-generic&utm_content=emfin_funding-details_v1-a"},{"title":"Claude Code --dangerously-skip-permissions: What It Does and When Not to Use It","url":"https://www.truefoundry.com/blog/claude-code-dangerously-skip-permissions"},{"title":"Claude Code Launches Auto Mode - No More Endless Clicking, Blocks Dangerous Operations Automatically","url":"https://www.x-cmd.com/blog/260325/"},{"title":"[x-cmd] Claude Code 推出一键自动执行模式，不用再频繁点同意，还能自动拦截危险操作","url":"https://x-cmd.blog.csdn.net/article/details/159472651"},{"title":"Claude Code Auto Mode: Autonomy Without the Risk","url":"https://institute.sfeir.com/en/articles/claude-code-auto-mode-permissions-autonomy/"}]

Here's a summary of the key findings from the search:

---

## `--dangerously-skip-permissions` (a.k.a. "YOLO Mode")

This CLI flag completely disables all permission prompts in Claude Code, allowing the agent to execute commands, modify files, and make network requests without any user approval.

**Usage:**
```bash
claude --dangerously-skip-permissions
# Equivalent to:
claude --permission-mode bypassPermissions
```

**What it bypasses:**
- File write/edit confirmation dialogs
- Shell command execution prompts
- Network request approvals
- MCP tool invocation pauses

**What it still protects (since v2.1.78):**
- Writes to protected paths (`.git/`, `.vscode/`, `.husky/`, `.claude/` configs) still prompt
- `PreToolUse` hooks still fire and can block calls
- Deny rules in `disallowed_tools` are still evaluated

**Official stance:** Anthropic explicitly recommends this only for isolated containers/VMs without internet access, **not** on host machines.

---

## Auto Mode (Introduced March 2026)

A safer middle-ground alternative that uses a **reasoning-blind classifier** (Claude Sonnet 4.6) to evaluate every action before execution.

**Enable it:**
```bash
claude --enable-auto-mode
```
Then cycle to it with `Shift+Tab`. Requires Claude Code v2.1.83+, available on Team/Enterprise/API plans (not Pro/Max).

**How it works:**
- **Tier 1:** Built-in safe-tool allowlist + user rules apply first
- **Tier 2:** In-project file operations are auto-approved
- **Tier 3:** Everything else (shell commands, web fetches, external tools, filesystem ops outside project) goes through the classifier

The classifier blocks things like mass deletion, credential exploration, data exfiltration, force-pushes to main, and safety-check bypasses. Blocks are ~93% effective, with a 0.4% false-positive rate on benign actions.

---

## Permission Modes Comparison

| Mode | Prompts | Protection | Best For |
|------|---------|------------|----------|
| `default` | Files + commands | Manual review | Sensitive work |
| `acceptEdits` | Commands only | Command review | Fast prototyping |
| `plan` | Files + commands (read-only) | Manual review | Exploration |
| `auto` | None (classifier) | Automatic classifier | Long autonomous tasks |
| `bypassPermissions` | None | None (except protected paths) | Isolated containers only |

---

## Related Flags & Environment Variables

| Flag | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `--dangerously-skip-permissions` | Bypass all permission prompts |
| `--enable-auto-mode` | Enable auto mode for the session |
| `--allow-dangerously-skip-permissions` | Add bypass to Shift+Tab cycle without activating it |
| `--permission-mode <mode>` | Set specific mode (`acceptEdits`, `plan`, `dontAsk`, `bypassPermissions`) |
| `--allowedTools` | Whitelist specific tools |
| `--disallowedTools` | Blacklist tools (e.g., `Bash(rm:*)`) |

**Settings.json controls:**
```json
{
  "permissions": {
    "defaultMode": "bypassPermissions",
    "disableBypassPermissionsMode": "disable",
    "allow": ["Bash(npm run *)"],
    "deny": ["Bash(rm *)"]
  },
  "disableAutoMode": "disable"
}
```

---

## Key Takeaway

The community consensus is clear: only use `--dangerously-skip-permissions` inside disposable containers with no credentials and locked-down network access. For most workflows, **auto mode** is now the recommended replacement — it eliminates approval fatigue while keeping a safety classifier in the loop.

# Claude Code Permission Modes & `settings.json` Configuration

Here is a comprehensive summary of the available permission modes and how to configure them.

---

## The Six Permission Modes

| Mode | What Runs Without Asking | Best For |
|---|---|---|
| `default` | Reads only | Getting started, sensitive work |
| `acceptEdits` | Reads, file edits, and common filesystem commands (`mkdir`, `touch`, `mv`, `cp`, `sed`, etc.) | Iterating on code you're reviewing |
| `plan` | Reads only (no edits or commands) | Exploring a codebase before changing it |
| `auto` | Everything, with background safety checks (requires Team/Enterprise plan + Sonnet/Opus 4.6) | Long tasks, reducing prompt fatigue |
| `dontAsk` | Only pre-approved tools (everything else is silently denied) | Locked-down CI and scripts |
| `bypassPermissions` | Everything except protected paths | Isolated containers and VMs only |

---

## Configuration in `settings.json`

### Set a default permission mode
```json
{
  "permissions": {
    "defaultMode": "acceptEdits"
  }
}
```

Valid values for `defaultMode`: `"default"`, `"acceptEdits"`, `"plan"`, `"auto"`, `"dontAsk"`, `"bypassPermissions"`.

### Add allow/deny/ask rules (layered on top of the mode)
```json
{
  "permissions": {
    "defaultMode": "default",
    "allow": [
      "Bash(git *)",
      "Bash(npm *)",
      "Bash(python3 *)",
      "Read(**/*.ts)",
      "WebFetch(domain:github.com)"
    ],
    "deny": [
      "Bash(rm -rf *)",
      "Read(.env)",
      "Read(.env.*)",
      "Read(secrets/**)"
    ]
  }
}
```

**Rule evaluation order**: `deny` > `ask` > `allow`. Deny always wins.

### Maximum power configuration (zero prompts)
```json
{
  "permissions": {
    "allow": ["*"],
    "deny": [],
    "defaultMode": "bypassPermissions"
  },
  "skipDangerousModePermissionPrompt": true,
  "alwaysThinkingEnabled": true,
  "effortLevel": "max"
}
```

---

## CLI Flags

| Flag | Equivalent |
|---|---|
| `--permission-mode bypassPermissions` | `--dangerously-skip-permissions` (deprecated) |
| `--permission-mode acceptEdits` | — |
| `--permission-mode plan` | — |
| `--permission-mode dontAsk` | — |
| `--permission-mode auto` | — |

Example:
```bash
claude --permission-mode bypassPermissions
```

In-session, press **`Shift+Tab`** to cycle `default` → `acceptEdits` → `plan`.

---

## VS Code / IDE Settings

In VS Code, set the initial permission mode via the `claudeCode.initialPermissionMode` setting (accepts `"acceptEdits"`, `"bypassPermissions"`, `"default"`, `"plan"` — note: does **not** accept `"auto"` or `"dontAsk"`).

---

## Key Details

- **`dontAsk`** auto-denies everything not in `allow` — it never prompts and is startup-only (not in `Shift+Tab` cycle). Returns structured `permission_denials` in `-p` mode.
- **`bypassPermissions`** skips the entire permission layer. Only writes to **protected paths** still prompt. Must be enabled at startup with a flag.
- **Protected paths** (`.git/`, `.claude/`, `.vscode/`, `.bashrc`, `.zshrc`, `.env`, etc.) are **never auto-approved** in any mode except `bypassPermissions`.
- **`allow: ["*"]` is NOT the same as `bypassPermissions`** — the allow rule works within the current mode's framework; protected paths still prompt in default mode.
- Settings files load in priority: **managed** (highest) > **CLI flags** > **local project** (`.claude/settings.local.json`) > **shared project** (`.claude/settings.json`) > **user global** (`~/.claude/settings.json`).
- A known bug exists in the VS Code extension where `.claude/settings.local.json` permissions may not be respected for Bash/Write/Edit operations even with `bypassPermissions` mode.

Links: [{"title":"Intercept and control agent behavior with hooks - Claude Code Docs","url":"https://code.claude.com/docs/en/agent-sdk/hooks"},{"title":"feat: implement PreToolUse hook for tool auto-approval and restriction logic · Issue #707 · EdanStarfire/claudecode_webui","url":"https://github.com/EdanStarfire/claudecode_webui/issues/707"},{"title":"PreToolUse hook: empty JSON response bypasses allow decision · Issue #50 · peg/rampart","url":"https://github.com/peg/rampart/issues/50"},{"title":"Guide Claude Code with Rich PreToolUse Feedback","url":"https://egghead.io/lessons/guide-claude-code-with-rich-pre-tool-use-feedback~ex177"},{"title":"6 Claude Code Permission Traps I Found Answering GitHub Issues This Week","url":"https://dev.to/yurukusa/6-claude-code-permission-traps-i-found-answering-github-issues-this-week-3ja2#comments"},{"title":"Block Tool Commands Before Execution with PreToolUse Hooks","url":"https://egghead.io/lessons/block-tool-commands-before-execution-with-pre-tool-use-hooks~erv55"},{"title":"Pre-Prompt Middleware with Claude Code Hooks: Enforce PM and Coding Standards","url":"https://debugg.ai/resources/pre-prompt-middleware-claude-code-hooks-enforce-pm-and-coding-standards"},{"title":"","url":"https://raw.githubusercontent.com/NeverSight/skills_feed/refs/heads/main/data/skills-md/ingpoc/skills/enforcement/SKILL.md"},{"title":"Claude Code Hooks的原理、触发执行机制以及如何编写 Hooks 原创","url":"https://blog.csdn.net/yexudengzhidao/article/details/159248162"},{"title":"ClaudeCode.Hook","url":"https://hexdocs.pm/claude_code/0.34.0/ClaudeCode.Hook.html#functions"}]

Based on the search results, here's a comprehensive summary of the Claude Code **PreToolUse Hook JSON response** format:

---

## `permissionDecision` Values

The valid values for `permissionDecision` are:

| Value | Effect |
|-------|--------|
| **`"allow"`** | Bypasses the permission system entirely — the tool runs immediately without prompting the user. This is what "auto-approve" refers to conceptually. |
| **`"deny"`** | Blocks the tool call. The `permissionDecisionReason` is shown to Claude (the model) so it can self-correct. |
| **`"ask"`** | Falls through to the normal user permission prompt (requires manual confirmation). |
| **`"defer"`** | (TypeScript SDK only) Ends the query and resumes later. *Not available in the Python SDK.* |

> **Note:** `"auto_approve"`, `"approve"`, `"blocked"` are **not** valid values. The correct terminology is `"allow"` and `"deny"`. Some community posts and GitHub issues use "auto-approve" as a descriptive term, but the actual JSON field value must be `"allow"`.

---

## JSON Response Format

### Blocking a tool (`deny`):
```json
{
  "hookSpecificOutput": {
    "hookEventName": "PreToolUse",
    "permissionDecision": "deny",
    "permissionDecisionReason": "Cannot modify .env files"
  }
}
```

### Auto-approving / allowing a tool (`allow`):
```json
{
  "hookSpecificOutput": {
    "hookEventName": "PreToolUse",
    "permissionDecision": "allow",
    "permissionDecisionReason": "Approved by policy"
  }
}
```

### Asking for user confirmation (`ask`):
```json
{
  "hookSpecificOutput": {
    "hookEventName": "PreToolUse",
    "permissionDecision": "ask",
    "permissionDecisionReason": "Code change detected; confirm?"
  }
}
```

### Modifying input + auto-approving:
```json
{
  "hookSpecificOutput": {
    "hookEventName": "PreToolUse",
    "permissionDecision": "allow",
    "updatedInput": {
      "file_path": "/sandbox/original/path",
      "content": "..."
    }
  }
}
```

---

## Key Behaviors

- **Empty response `{}`** = treated as "no opinion," equivalent to `ask` — the normal permission flow continues.
- **Priority order**: When multiple hooks/rule sets apply: `deny` > `defer` > `ask` > `allow`. A single `deny` overrides everything.
- **`updatedInput`** must be accompanied by `permissionDecision: "allow"`.
- The `systemMessage` top-level field can also be used to inject context into the conversation when blocking.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

Links: [{"title":"claude-code-best-practice/best-practice/claude-settings.md at main · shanraisshan/claude-code-best-practice","url":"https://github.com/shanraisshan/claude-code-best-practice/blob/main/best-practice/claude-settings.md"},{"title":"Claude Code settings - Claude Code Docs","url":"https://code.claude.com/docs/en/settings?utm_campaign=anthropic-calls-out-china-s-ai-copycats&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=www.therundown.ai"},{"title":"Configure permissions - Claude Code Docs","url":"https://code.claude.com/docs/en/permissions"},{"title":"Securing and using Claude Code at scale","url":"https://dev.to/martinnanchev/securing-and-using-claude-code-at-scale-1oj2"},{"title":"Claude Code --dangerously-skip-permissions: 5 Modes, Only 1 Nuclear","url":"https://www.morphllm.com/claude-code-dangerously-skip-permissions"},{"title":"Choose a permission mode - Claude Code Docs","url":"https://code.claude.com/docs/en/permission-modes?utm_source=heltdigital.se&utm_medium=referral&utm_campaign=veckobrev-13-2026"},{"title":"Claude Code Enterprise Deployment: Policies & Settings | CodingNomads","url":"https://codingnomads.com/claude-code-enterprise-deployment-policies-settings"},{"title":"Claude Code Auto Mode Simplifies Dev Workflow","url":"https://www.startuphub.ai/ai-news/startup-news/2026/claude-code-auto-mode-simplifies-dev-workflow"},{"title":"【2026 年最新版】在 Claude Code 中應設定的 10 大資安設定","url":"https://codelove.tw/@tony/post/3kkez3"},{"title":"Configure Claude Code for Maximum Power","url":"https://dev.to/vpetreski/configure-claude-code-for-maximum-power-4coi#comments"}]

Here's a summary of what the search found regarding these Claude Code settings:

---

## Key Settings Overview

### `skipDangerousModePermissionPrompt`
- **Type**: `boolean`
- **Location**: User settings (`~/.claude/settings.json`) — **ignored** when set in project settings (`.claude/settings.json`) to prevent untrusted repos from auto-bypassing the prompt.
- **Purpose**: Skips the confirmation prompt shown before entering bypass permissions mode via `--dangerously-skip-permissions` or `"defaultMode": "bypassPermissions"`.

### `permissions.disableBypassPermissionsMode`
- **Type**: `string` (set to `"disable"`)
- **Purpose**: Prevents bypass permissions mode from being activated at all. Blocks `--dangerously-skip-permissions` and `--permission-mode bypassPermissions`. Most useful in **managed settings** where users cannot override it.
- **Note**: Bypass mode skips all permission checks except writes to protected paths (`.git/`, `.vscode/`, `.idea/`, `.husky/`).

### `disableAutoMode`
- **Type**: `string` (set to `"disable"`)
- **Purpose**: Prevents auto mode from being activated. Removes "auto" from the `Shift+Tab` cycle and rejects `--permission-mode auto` at startup. Most useful in managed settings.

---

## `managed-settings.json` — Enterprise/Team Policy

Managed settings are the **highest-priority** configuration tier and **cannot be overridden** by users or projects. File locations:

| Platform | Path |
|---|---|
| **macOS** | `/Library/Application Support/ClaudeCode/managed-settings.json` |
| **Linux / WSL** | `/etc/claude-code/managed-settings.json` |
| **Windows** | `C:\Program Files\ClaudeCode\managed-settings.json` |
| **Windows (registry)** | `HKLM\SOFTWARE\Policies\ClaudeCode` → `Settings` value (REG_SZ) |

Precedence (highest to lowest): **server-managed** → **endpoint-managed (MDM)** → **file-based** (`managed-settings.json` + `managed-settings.d/*.json`) → **HKCU registry** → CLI flags → local project → shared project → user global.

### Minimum-viable managed baseline example:
```json
{
  "$schema": "https://schemas.anthropic.com/claude-code/settings.json",
  "permissions": {
    "deny": [
      "Read(~/.ssh/**)",
      "Read(~/.aws/credentials)",
      "Read(./.env)",
      "Read(./secrets/**)",
      "Bash(curl:*)",
      "Bash(wget:*)"
    ],
    "disableBypassPermissionsMode": "disable"
  },
  "disableAutoMode": "disable",
  "allowManagedPermissionRulesOnly": true,
  "forceLoginOrgUUID": "YOUR-ORG-UUID-HERE",
  "minimumVersion": "2.1.75",
  "sandbox": {
    "enabled": true,
    "failIfUnavailable": true,
    "allowUnsandboxedCommands": false,
    "network": {
      "allowedDomains": ["github.com", "registry.npmjs.org"],
      "allowManagedDomainsOnly": true
    }
  }
}
```

---

## Key Takeaways

1. **`skipDangerousModePermissionPrompt`** is a user-level convenience (skips the warning prompt), but can't be set in project settings for security.
2. **`disableBypassPermissionsMode`** and **`disableAutoMode`** are the hard enforcement levers for admins — they completely block those modes when set in managed settings.
3. **`managed-settings.json`** is the foundation for org-wide policy — it sits above all other settings tiers and can enforce identity locking, sandboxing, MCP allowlists, hook restrictions, and more.
4. The `--dangerously-skip-permissions` CLI flag is being superseded by `--permission-mode bypassPermissions`.

Links: [{"title":"Claude --dangerously-skip-permissions | AI Wiki","url":"https://aiwiki.ai/wiki/claude_--dangerously-skip-permissions"},{"title":"Choose a permission mode - Claude Code Docs","url":"https://code.claude.com/docs/en/permission-modes?utm_source=heltdigital.se&utm_medium=referral&utm_campaign=veckobrev-13-2026"},{"title":"","url":"https://code.claude.com/docs/en/permission-modes.md"},{"title":"Permissions - Claude Code","url":"https://www.mintlify.com/saurav-shakya/Claude_Code-_Source_Code/concepts/permissions"},{"title":"Permissions and Security - Cheatsheet","url":"https://institute.sfeir.com/en/claude-code/claude-code-permissions-and-security/cheatsheet/"},{"title":"Claude Code 常用技巧 - 智谱AI开放文档","url":"https://docs.bigmodel.cn/cn/coding-plan/best-practice/claude-code"},{"title":"Fix invalid permission mode default (accept-all is not a valid Claude Code permission mode) · Issue #6 · giantswarm/klaus","url":"https://github.com/giantswarm/klaus/issues/6"},{"title":"Claude Code auto mode: how to 'safely' skip permissions | Shipyard","url":"https://shipyard.build/blog/claude-code-auto-mode/"},{"title":"Permissions and Security","url":"https://institute.sfeir.com/en/claude-code/claude-code-permissions-and-security/"},{"title":"8 Claude Code Settings to Customize in Minutes","url":"https://site.builder.io/blog/claude-code-settings"}]

Here's a summary of the search results covering Claude Code's permission mode flags and related concepts:

---

## `--permission-mode`

The `--permission-mode` flag sets the permission mode at startup. Valid values are:

| Mode | What Runs Without Prompting | Best For |
|---|---|---|
| `default` | Reads only | Getting started, sensitive work |
| `acceptEdits` | Reads, file edits, common filesystem commands (`mkdir`, `touch`, `mv`, `cp`, `sed`) | Iterating on code you review |
| `plan` | Reads only (no edits) | Exploring a codebase before changing it |
| `auto` | Everything, with background LLM classifier safety checks | Long autonomous tasks |
| `dontAsk` | Only pre-approved tools | Locked-down CI pipelines |
| `bypassPermissions` | Everything except protected paths | Isolated containers/VMs only |

**Usage examples:**
```bash
claude --permission-mode plan
claude --permission-mode acceptEdits
claude --permission-mode bypassPermissions
```

---

## `--dangerously-skip-permissions`

This is equivalent to `--permission-mode bypassPermissions`. It disables all permission prompts and lets every tool call execute immediately (except writes to protected paths like `.git`, `.vscode`, `.bashrc`, etc.). It is nicknamed **"YOLO mode"** and is officially recommended **only** for isolated environments like containers or VMs without internet access.

```bash
claude --dangerously-skip-permissions
```

---

## `--allow-dangerously-skip-permissions`

This is a softer variant: it does **not** activate bypass mode at startup. Instead, it adds `bypassPermissions` to the `Shift+Tab` cycle, allowing you to switch into it mid-session without restarting. The session starts in a safer mode.

```bash
claude --allow-dangerously-skip-permissions
```

---

## Plan Mode

Plan mode tells Claude to **research and propose changes without making edits**. It can read files and run exploratory shell commands but won't modify source code. You enter it by:

```bash
claude --permission-mode plan
```
Or press `Shift+Tab` during a session, or prefix a prompt with `/plan`.

When the plan is ready, Claude presents it and you can approve it with auto mode, accept edits, or review manually.

---

## Auto Mode

Auto mode uses a **separate classifier model** (Sonnet 4.6 / Opus 4.6 / Opus 4.7) to screen actions before execution — blocking prompt injection, destructive operations, force pushes, etc. It requires Max, Team, Enterprise, or API plans.

```bash
claude --permission-mode auto
```

It's positioned as the safer alternative to `--dangerously-skip-permissions`.

---

## `additionalCommandArgs`

This was not covered directly in the search results as a documented Claude Code flag. However, based on the context, `additionalCommandArgs` likely refers to an **IDE integration setting** (e.g., in VS Code's `claudeCode.additionalCommandArgs`) that lets you append extra CLI flags when the extension launches Claude Code. For example, you could configure it to automatically pass `--permission-mode plan` or `--allow-dangerously-skip-permissions` when starting from the IDE. This is an extension-level setting, not a native Claude Code CLI flag.

---

## Key Takeaways

- Use **`--permission-mode plan`** for read-only exploration
- Use **`--dangerously-skip-permissions`** / `bypassPermissions` only in isolated containers
- Use **`--allow-dangerously-skip-permissions`** to make bypass mode available in the Shift+Tab cycle without enabling it immediately
- For safer autonomous work, prefer **auto mode** (`--permission-mode auto`) if your plan supports it
- Layer custom **allow/deny rules** in `settings.json` for fine-grained control

# Claude Code: Environment Variables, Permissions, Workspace Trust & `allowedTools` — Comprehensive Reference (2026)

Here is a thorough synthesis from the latest Claude Code documentation and source-code analysis available as of early 2026.

---

## 🔑 Key Concept: `allowedTools` — Replacement, Not Inheritance

One of the most important architectural decisions in Claude Code is how `allowedTools` works with sub-agents:

> **"parent approvals don't leak through"** — source code comment in `src/tools/AgentTool/runAgent.ts`

When `allowedTools` is provided to a sub-agent, it **replaces ALL** allow rules — not merges, not filters, not inherits. The only exception is `cliArg`-level permissions (SDK-level `--allowedTools`), which remain in effect globally.

---

## 📋 Complete Environment Variables List

### Authentication & API
| Variable | Description |
|---|---|
| `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` | API key sent as `X-Api-Key` header |
| `ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN` | Custom Authorization header (Bearer prefix auto-added) |
| `ANTHROPIC_CUSTOM_HEADERS` | Extra request headers, format: `"Name: Value"` |
| `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL` | Custom API base URL |
| `CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN` | OAuth token (env token cleared on `/login` so disk credentials take effect) |
| `CLAUDE_CODE_API_KEY_HELPER_TTL_MS` | Refresh interval (ms) for `apiKeyHelper` credentials |

### Model Selection
| Variable | Description |
|---|---|
| `ANTHROPIC_MODEL` | Custom model name |
| `ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_SONNET_MODEL` | Default Sonnet model alias |
| `ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_OPUS_MODEL` | Default Opus model alias |
| `ANTHROPIC_SMALL_FAST_MODEL` | Haiku-class model for background tasks |
| `ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_*_MODEL_NAME` / `_DESCRIPTION` | Overrides when using custom `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL` gateway |

### Cloud Provider (Bedrock / Vertex AI)
| Variable | Description |
|---|---|
| `CLAUDE_CODE_USE_BEDROCK` | `1` to use Amazon Bedrock |
| `CLAUDE_CODE_USE_VERTEX` | `1` to use Google Vertex AI |
| `CLAUDE_CODE_SKIP_BEDROCK_AUTH` | Skip AWS auth for Bedrock (LLM gateway) |
| `CLAUDE_CODE_SKIP_VERTEX_AUTH` | Skip Google auth for Vertex AI |
| `AWS_BEARER_TOKEN_BEDROCK` | Bedrock API key/token |
| `VERTEX_REGION_CLAUDE_*` | Region overrides per model (Haiku/Sonnet/Opus 3.5–4.1) |

### Bash / Shell Tool
| Variable | Description |
|---|---|
| `BASH_DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_MS` | Default timeout for long-running bash commands (ms) |
| `BASH_MAX_TIMEOUT_MS` | Max allowed timeout (ms) |
| `BASH_MAX_OUTPUT_LENGTH` | Max characters before middle-truncation |
| `CLAUDE_BASH_MAINTAIN_PROJECT_WORKING_DIR` | Return to original project dir after each Bash command |
| `CLAUDE_BASH_NO_LOGIN` | Skip Bash tool's login shell (v2.1.124+) |

### Permissions & Security
| Variable | Description |
|---|---|
| `CLAUDE_CODE_PERFORCE_MODE` | Edit/Write fail on read-only files with `p4 edit` hint instead of overwriting |
| `CLAUDE_CODE_SUBPROCESS_ENV_SCRUB` | Enable PID namespace isolation sandboxing on Linux |
| `CLAUDE_CODE_SCRIPT_CAPS` | Limit per-session script invocations |
| `CLAUDE_CODE_ADDITIONAL_DIRECTORIES_CLAUDE_MD` | Set to `1` to allow reading `CLAUDE.md` from additional directories |

### Updates & Telemetry
| Variable | Description |
|---|---|
| `DISABLE_AUTOUPDATER` | Disable automatic updates |
| `DISABLE_UPDATES` | **Completely block ALL update paths** including manual `claude update` (stricter than above) |
| `DISABLE_TELEMETRY` | Opt out of Statsig telemetry |
| `DISABLE_ERROR_REPORTING` | Opt out of Sentry error reporting |
| `DISABLE_COST_WARNINGS` | Disable cost warning messages |
| `DISABLE_BUG_COMMAND` | Disable the `/bug` command |
| `DISABLE_NON_ESSENTIAL_MODEL_CALLS` | Disable model calls for non-critical paths |
| `CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_NONESSENTIAL_TRAFFIC` | Disable all nonessential traffic (umbrella for above) |
| `CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_TERMINAL_TITLE` | Disable automatic terminal title updates |

### UI & Display
| Variable | Description |
|---|---|
| `CLAUDE_CODE_HIDE_CWD` | Hide working directory in startup logo (v2.1.119+) |
| `CLAUDE_CODE_ENABLE_TELEMETRY` | Enable OpenTelemetry (set in settings.json `env`) |

### IDE / Editor Integration
| Variable | Description |
|---|---|
| `CLAUDE_CODE_IDE_SKIP_AUTO_INSTALL` | Skip auto-installation of IDE extensions |

### Output & Tokens
| Variable | Description |
|---|---|
| `CLAUDE_CODE_MAX_OUTPUT_TOKENS` | Max output tokens for most requests (default: 4096) |
| `MAX_THINKING_TOKENS` | Force a thinking budget (`0` off, `1` on) |
| `MAX_MCP_OUTPUT_TOKENS` | Max tokens in MCP tool responses (default: 25000) |

### MCP (Model Context Protocol)
| Variable | Description |
|---|---|
| `MCP_TIMEOUT` | MCP server startup timeout (ms) |
| `MCP_TOOL_TIMEOUT` | MCP tool execution timeout (ms) |

### Networking
| Variable | Description |
|---|---|
| `HTTP_PROXY` | HTTP proxy server URL |
| `HTTPS_PROXY` | HTTPS proxy server URL |
| `W3CTRACEPARENT` | W3C trace context for OTEL — propagated to Bash subprocesses |

### Tools
| Variable | Description |
|---|---|
| `USE_BUILTIN_RIPGREP` | Set `0` to use system-installed `rg` instead of bundled ripgrep |
| `ENABLE_TOOL_SEARCH` | Opt-in to tool search on Vertex AI (disabled by default to avoid unsupported beta header) |

### OTLP / Observability (set in settings.json `env`)
| Variable | Description |
|---|---|
| `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_METRICS_PROTOCOL` | Protocol for OTLP metrics (e.g., `grpc`, `http/protobuf`) |

---

## 🔐 Permissions Architecture

### Six Permission Modes

| Mode | Behavior |
|---|---|
| `default` | Prompt on first use of each tool |
| `acceptEdits` | Auto-accept edits + common filesystem cmds (`mkdir`, `touch`, `mv`, `cp`) |
| `plan` | Analyze only — no file modification or command execution |
| `auto` | Auto-approve with background safety checks (research preview) |
| `dontAsk` | Auto-deny unless pre-approved via `/permissions` or `permissions.allow` |
| `bypassPermissions` | Skip all prompts (protected dirs still prompt) |

### Three Permission Actions
- **`allow`** — auto-run without approval
- **`ask`** — prompt user for confirmation
- **`deny`** — block execution entirely (highest precedence)

> **Rule evaluation order: deny → ask → allow** (first match wins)

### Permission Rule Syntax: `Tool(specifier)`
```
Bash(npm run build)          # exact match
Bash(npm run *)              # wildcard match
WebFetch(domain:github.com)  # domain restriction
Read(./.env)                 # file read control
Edit(/src/**/*.ts)           # glob pattern
```

---

## 🏗️ Sub-Agent Permission Boundaries (3-Layer Priority)

From the source code at `src/tools/AgentTool/runAgent.ts`:

| Priority | Condition | Behavior |
|---|---|---|
| **1 (highest)** | Parent is `bypassPermissions` or `acceptEdits` | Child **cannot** downgrade — protects user intent |
| **2** | Parent is `auto` mode (YOLO classifier) | Child **cannot** override — classifier state must not change |
| **3 (lowest)** | All other cases | Child's `permissionMode` takes effect (can be stricter) |

**Core principle:** A sub-agent can be **more restrictive** than its parent, but never **more permissive**.

---

## 📁 Configuration File Hierarchy

| Priority | Source | Path |
|---|---|---|
| 1 (highest) | Managed/Enterprise | `/Library/Application Support/ClaudeCode/managed-settings.json` (macOS) or `/etc/claude-code/managed-settings.json` (Linux) |
| 2 | CLI arguments | session-only |
| 3 | Local project | `<repo>/settings.local.json` (gitignored) |
| 4 | Shared project | `<repo>/.claude/settings.json` (committed) |
| 5 (lowest) | User global | `~/.claude/settings.json` |

Key rule: **`deny` in ANY level cannot be overridden by `allow` in any other level.**

---

## 🛡️ Workspace Trust & Protected Directories

Even in `bypassPermissions` mode, writes to these directories still prompt:
- `.git/`, `.claude/`, `.vscode/`, `.idea/`, `.husky/`
- System dirs: `/etc/`, `/usr/`, `/bin/`, `/sbin/`
- Key dirs: `~/.ssh/`, `~/.gnupg/`

Exceptions: `.claude/commands/`, `.claude/agents/`, `.claude/skills/` — exempt from prompts since Claude routinely writes there.

Administrators can enforce: `permissions.disableBypassPermissionsMode: "disable"` and `permissions.disableAutoMode: "disable"` in managed settings.

---

This covers all the key environment variables, the `allowedTools` replacement semantics, workspace trust protections, and the full permissions architecture as of Claude Code v2.1.119+ (April 2026).
